content: Just finished 'Project Hail Mary' by Andy Weir. Absolutely brilliant! Have you read it?
I loved that book too! The science was so well-researched and the story was gripping. What did you think of the ending?
The ending got me right in the feels. I might have teared up a bit. Speaking of science, I've been really into 3D printing lately. Built my own printer last year.
That's impressive! What kind of things do you like to print?
Mostly practical stuff for my business - custom electrical component holders, tool organizers, that kind of thing. I run my own electrical contracting business in Chicago.
That's really smart using 3D printing for your business. Do you find it saves you money in the long run?

Definitely. The initial investment paid for itself within a few months. Plus it's just fun to design and create things. I've also been getting into home brewing beer - made a pretty good IPA last month.
Home brewing sounds like a great hobby! How did you get started with that?
Started during the pandemic when I had more time at home. I've always loved craft beer, so I figured why not try making my own? It's been a fun learning process.
That's awesome. Do you have a favorite style you like to brew or drink?
I'm really into IPAs and stouts. For brewing, I like experimenting with different hop combinations. Oh, and I've got this massive aquarium setup at home - it's become quite the obsession.
An aquarium sounds amazing! What kind of fish do you keep?

Mostly freshwater - I've got a school of neon tetras, some angelfish, and a few corydoras. It's incredibly relaxing to watch them. I've also started beekeeping recently - got two hives in my backyard.
Beekeeping is fascinating! How's that going? I've heard it can be quite challenging.
It's been a learning curve but really rewarding. Harvested my first batch of honey last month - it's so much better than store-bought. I'm thinking of expanding to three hives next year.
That's incredible! Fresh honey from your own bees must be amazing. Do you use it in your home brewing at all?

Not yet, but that's a great idea! I've been using it mostly in my morning coffee. Speaking of which, I should probably get back to work - I've got an electrical inspection scheduled for this afternoon.
Good luck with the inspection! It sounds like you've got a really interesting mix of hobbies and a successful business. Let me know if you ever want to swap home brewing recipes!
